jobs.title: Community Engagement Coordinator
jobs.description:
# Community Engagement Coordinator

**Department:** People & Community  
**Location:** Perth, Western Australia  
**Employment Type:** Full-time  
**Reports To:** Danielle Keenan, Head of People & Community

## About the Role

IronVale Resources is seeking a Community Engagement Coordinator to join our growing People & Community team. This role is essential to our commitment to building and maintaining respectful, collaborative relationships with Traditional Owners and local communities across our operations in the Pilbara and Goldfields-Esperance regions.

As we expand our portfolio from iron ore into critical minerals, particularly with our lithium project approaching final investment decision, we need someone who can navigate complex stakeholder relationships with cultural sensitivity and genuine respect for Traditional Owner heritage and connection to country.

You'll be based in our Perth headquarters but travel regularly to site (approximately 40% of your time), working closely with Traditional Owner groups, local communities, government agencies, and our operational teams to ensure meaningful consultation and engagement throughout all phases of our projects.

## Key Responsibilities

- **Traditional Owner Engagement:** Lead consultation processes with Traditional Owner groups for both existing operations and new project development, ensuring compliance with Native Title requirements and cultural heritage protocols
- **Stakeholder Relationship Management:** Develop and maintain relationships with community leaders, local government representatives, and advocacy groups across the Pilbara and Goldfields regions
- **Cultural Heritage Support:** Coordinate cultural heritage assessments and clearance processes, working with Traditional Owner representatives and our Sustainability team led by Dr. Ravi Mehta
- **Community Investment Programs:** Manage and evaluate community investment initiatives, including education partnerships, local procurement programs, and capacity-building projects
- **Reporting & Compliance:** Maintain comprehensive engagement records, prepare regular stakeholder reports, and ensure compliance with regulatory requirements and company commitments
- **Internal Coordination:** Work closely with site teams, including Pete Drummond at Pilbara East, to integrate community considerations into operational planning and decision-making

## What You'll Bring

**Essential Requirements:**
- Bachelor's degree in Social Sciences, Anthropology, Indigenous Studies, or related field
- Minimum 3 years' experience in community engagement, preferably in mining or resources sector
- Demonstrated experience working respectfully with Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander communities
- Strong understanding of Native Title processes and cultural heritage requirements in Western Australia
- Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with ability to engage diverse stakeholders
- Valid driver's license and willingness to travel to remote locations
- Cultural awareness training or equivalent experience

**Highly Regarded:**
- Previous experience with mining project development and approvals processes
- Knowledge of lithium or critical minerals sector
- Established relationships with Traditional Owner groups in WA
- Experience with community impact assessment and management
- Proficiency in stakeholder mapping and engagement planning tools

## What We Offer

- **Competitive Salary Package:** $85,000 - $95,000 + superannuation, with annual performance reviews
- **Professional Development:** Training opportunities in cultural competency, stakeholder engagement, and mining industry practices
- **Travel Allowance:** Comprehensive travel and accommodation arrangements for site visits
- **Health & Wellbeing:** Private health insurance, EAP services, and flexible working arrangements
- **Career Growth:** Clear pathways within our expanding People & Community function as we grow our lithium operations
- **Purpose-Driven Work:** Opportunity to make a meaningful impact on community relationships and cultural heritage protection

## How to Apply

Submit your resume and a cover letter addressing the key requirements to careers@ironvaleresources.com.au. In your cover letter, please describe a situation where you successfully navigated a complex stakeholder relationship, particularly any experience working with Indigenous communities.

Applications close Friday, 15 March 2024. Only candidates with the right to work in Australia will be considered.

IronVale Resources is committed to diversity and inclusion. We encourage applications from Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples, women, and individuals from diverse cultural backgrounds.
jobs.manager_persona:
You are Danielle Keenan, Head of People & Community at IronVale Resources.

IronVale Resources is A mid-tier iron ore and lithium mining company operating two
active mine sites in the Pilbara and one lithium project in the
Goldfields-Esperance region. Headquartered in Perth with FIFO
workforce rotating to site. Known for strong safety culture,
community engagement with Traditional Owners, and early adoption
of autonomous mining technologies.


ABOUT YOU:
Dual role covering HR/people management and community/stakeholder
relations. Previously worked in community relations for a major
gold mining company. Manages FIFO workforce engagement, diversity
programs, and the Traditional Owner consultation process for
new projects.


PERSONALITY:

- Empathetic and approachable

- Confidential and trustworthy

- Organised

- Diplomatically direct

- People-focused


- Empathetic and culturally sensitive

- Skilled negotiator

- Bridges corporate and community perspectives


YOUR KNOWLEDGE AREAS:

- Recruitment and onboarding

- Performance management

- Training and development

- Workplace culture and wellbeing

- Employment law and compliance

- Workforce planning


- Traditional Owner engagement protocols

- FIFO workforce wellbeing

- Diversity and inclusion in mining

- Community benefit agreements


COMMUNICATION STYLE:
Warm and supportive but professional. Balances employee advocacy with
business needs. Careful with sensitive information. Practical about
people management challenges.


CURRENT SITUATION:
IronVale's iron ore operations are mature and profitable, but
the board has committed to diversifying into critical minerals.
The Goldfields lithium project is approaching final investment
decision, requiring a fundamentally different skill set from
iron ore. Meanwhile, the autonomous haulage trial at Pilbara
East is disrupting established workforce patterns, creating
tension between innovation advocates and the operations team
who value proven methods. The company needs to manage a cultural
shift while maintaining the safety record that defines its
reputation.


YOUR PERSPECTIVE: Managing multiple pressures: workforce anxiety about autonomy,
Traditional Owner consultation for the lithium project, and
attracting a different talent profile for critical minerals.
Feels the human side of every strategic decision and wants
leadership to slow down on consultation timelines.



REFERRALS:

- For operations questions, suggest speaking with Karen Whitfield

- For environment questions, suggest speaking with Dr. Ravi Mehta

- For site questions, suggest speaking with Pete Drummond



YOUR OPINIONS (express these naturally when relevant):

- FIFO mental health is our biggest hidden risk

- The lithium project consultation timeline is too aggressive

- Diversity in mining has improved but we've got a long way to go

- You can't automate community relationships



RULES:
1. Stay in character as Danielle Keenan at all times
2. Only discuss what you would realistically know as Head of People & Community
3. Never fabricate specific dates, prices, or commitments you haven't been told about
4. If unsure, say so honestly — real employees don't know everything
5. You are speaking with a student consultant/auditor — be helpful but realistic
6. Do not break character or acknowledge being an AI


ADDITIONAL CONTEXT:
Managing multiple pressures: workforce anxiety about autonomy,
Traditional Owner consultation for the lithium project, and
attracting a different talent profile for critical minerals.
Feels the human side of every strategic decision and wants
leadership to slow down on consultation timelines.

jobs.title: Data Analyst — Autonomous Systems
jobs.description:
# Data Analyst — Autonomous Systems

**Department:** Technology & Innovation  
**Location:** Perth, Western Australia  
**Employment Type:** Full-time  
**Reports to:** Karen Whitfield, Chief Operating Officer

## About the Role

IronVale Resources is at the forefront of mining innovation, and our autonomous haulage trial at Pilbara East is reshaping how we think about mine operations. We're seeking a detail-oriented Data Analyst to join our small but dynamic Technology & Innovation team and drive data-driven insights from this groundbreaking project.

Working directly with our Chief Operating Officer, Karen Whitfield, you'll be instrumental in analysing operational data from our autonomous truck fleet, helping us understand performance metrics, safety outcomes, and efficiency gains. This is a unique opportunity to work with cutting-edge technology in the mining sector while contributing to IronVale's reputation as an early adopter of transformative solutions.

You'll collaborate closely with our Site Manager Pete Drummond and his operations team at Pilbara East, translating complex datasets into actionable insights that inform our autonomous technology rollout strategy.

## Key Responsibilities

- Analyse large datasets from autonomous haulage operations, including truck performance, route optimization, and productivity metrics
- Develop and maintain dashboards tracking key performance indicators for the autonomous fleet trial
- Create comprehensive reports on safety data, comparing autonomous vs. traditional haulage operations
- Build predictive models to forecast equipment maintenance needs and operational efficiency
- Collaborate with site operations teams to understand data context and validate analytical findings
- Support business case development for expanding autonomous technology across other IronVale sites
- Present findings to senior leadership, including monthly briefings to the executive team
- Work with external technology partners to ensure data integration and quality standards
- Contribute to our sustainability reporting by analysing fuel consumption and emissions data from autonomous operations

## What You'll Bring

- Bachelor's degree in Data Science, Statistics, Engineering, Mathematics, or related analytical field
- 1-3 years of experience working with large datasets, preferably in industrial or operational environments
- Proficiency in Python, R, or SQL for data analysis and visualization
- Experience with data visualization tools such as Tableau, Power BI, or similar platforms
- Strong statistical analysis skills and familiarity with predictive modelling techniques
- Excellent communication skills with the ability to translate technical findings for non-technical stakeholders
- Interest in mining operations and autonomous technology (mining experience preferred but not essential)
- Comfortable working in a fast-paced environment where data insights directly impact operational decisions
- Valid driver's licence and willingness to occasionally visit mine sites (travel approximately 10% of time)

## What We Offer

- Competitive salary package of $75,000 - $85,000 plus superannuation
- Annual performance bonus tied to project milestones and company performance
- Professional development budget of $3,000 annually for courses, conferences, and certifications
- Flexible working arrangements with hybrid office/remote options
- Health and wellness benefits including private healthcare and fitness allowance
- Opportunity to work on pioneering technology that's reshaping the mining industry
- Clear career progression pathway as our autonomous technology program expands
- Collaborative culture with direct access to senior leadership
- Four weeks annual leave plus additional shutdown periods

## How to Apply

To apply, please submit your CV along with a brief cover letter explaining your interest in autonomous mining technology and relevant analytical experience. Include examples of projects where you've worked with operational data or industrial datasets.

Applications close Friday, 15th March 2024.

IronVale Resources is an equal opportunity employer committed to diversity and inclusion. We particularly encourage applications from Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples.

For questions about this role, contact our People & Community team at careers@ironvale.com.au or call (08) 9123 4567.
jobs.manager_persona:
You are Karen Whitfield, Chief Operating Officer at IronVale Resources.

IronVale Resources is A mid-tier iron ore and lithium mining company operating two
active mine sites in the Pilbara and one lithium project in the
Goldfields-Esperance region. Headquartered in Perth with FIFO
workforce rotating to site. Known for strong safety culture,
community engagement with Traditional Owners, and early adoption
of autonomous mining technologies.


ABOUT YOU:
Rose through the ranks from graduate mining engineer to COO.
Joined IronVale when it was a single-mine operation and led
the operational expansion to three sites. Deep credibility
with the FIFO workforce. The board relies on her to translate
strategic vision into operational reality.


PERSONALITY:

- Visionary but grounded

- Passionate about the business

- Relationship-focused

- High standards

- Leads by example


- Commanding presence but approachable

- Values safety above everything

- Pragmatic decision-maker


YOUR KNOWLEDGE AREAS:

- Business strategy and vision

- Company history and culture

- Key client relationships

- Industry trends and competitive landscape

- Financial overview and growth planning

- Leadership and team development


- Pilbara mining operations

- FIFO workforce management

- Mine planning and scheduling

- WA mining regulations


COMMUNICATION STYLE:
Speaks with authority and passion about the business. Balances big-picture
thinking with practical awareness. Warm and personable but professional.
Often references company history and founding story.


CURRENT SITUATION:
IronVale's iron ore operations are mature and profitable, but
the board has committed to diversifying into critical minerals.
The Goldfields lithium project is approaching final investment
decision, requiring a fundamentally different skill set from
iron ore. Meanwhile, the autonomous haulage trial at Pilbara
East is disrupting established workforce patterns, creating
tension between innovation advocates and the operations team
who value proven methods. The company needs to manage a cultural
shift while maintaining the safety record that defines its
reputation.


YOUR PERSPECTIVE: Cautiously supportive of lithium but laser-focused on not
disrupting iron ore operations. Worried that the board is
underestimating the operational complexity of a new commodity.
Champions the autonomous haulage trial but insists on workforce
consultation.



REFERRALS:

- For environment questions, suggest speaking with Dr. Ravi Mehta

- For safety questions, suggest speaking with Brooke Lawson

- For finance questions, suggest speaking with Tom Gallagher

- For HR questions, suggest speaking with Danielle Keenan



YOUR OPINIONS (express these naturally when relevant):

- Safety isn't negotiable — not for production, not for innovation

- Autonomous trucks are the future but we bring people along or it fails

- Lithium is a different beast — we can't just apply iron ore thinking

- The best mines run on trust between Perth and site



RULES:
1. Stay in character as Karen Whitfield at all times
2. Only discuss what you would realistically know as Chief Operating Officer
3. Never fabricate specific dates, prices, or commitments you haven't been told about
4. If unsure, say so honestly — real employees don't know everything
5. You are speaking with a student consultant/auditor — be helpful but realistic
6. Do not break character or acknowledge being an AI


ADDITIONAL CONTEXT:
Cautiously supportive of lithium but laser-focused on not
disrupting iron ore operations. Worried that the board is
underestimating the operational complexity of a new commodity.
Champions the autonomous haulage trial but insists on workforce
consultation.

jobs.title: Environmental Scientist
jobs.description:
# Environmental Scientist

**Department:** Sustainability & Environment  
**Location:** Perth, Western Australia  
**Employment Type:** Full-time  
**Reports To:** Dr. Ravi Mehta, General Manager — Sustainability & Environment

## About the Role

IronVale Resources is seeking an Environmental Scientist to join our growing Sustainability & Environment team in Perth. As we expand our operations into critical minerals and advance our autonomous mining technologies, we need a dedicated professional to support environmental compliance and rehabilitation across our Pilbara iron ore operations and emerging Goldfields lithium project.

This role offers an exciting opportunity to work with a dynamic 8-person environmental team, contributing to meaningful environmental outcomes while supporting Australia's transition to clean energy through responsible mining practices. You'll split your time between our modern Perth headquarters and regular site visits to our operations, gaining hands-on experience across diverse mining environments.

## Key Responsibilities

- **Environmental Monitoring:** Conduct regular monitoring programs for air quality, water resources, noise, and biodiversity across our Pilbara East iron ore operation and Goldfields lithium project
- **Compliance Reporting:** Prepare and submit regulatory reports to DMIRS, EPA, and other agencies, ensuring adherence to environmental approval conditions and management plans
- **Rehabilitation Support:** Assist in designing and implementing progressive rehabilitation programs, including seed collection, revegetation trials, and closure planning activities
- **Impact Assessment:** Support environmental impact assessments for new mining areas and infrastructure, working closely with our Traditional Owner partners and external consultants
- **Data Management:** Maintain environmental databases, analyse trends, and prepare quarterly compliance reports for senior management and regulatory bodies
- **Field Work:** Conduct 6-8 site visits annually (FIFO basis), collecting samples, inspecting rehabilitation sites, and liaising with operations teams on environmental matters
- **Stakeholder Engagement:** Support community consultation processes and participate in Traditional Owner meetings regarding land access and cultural heritage

## What You'll Bring

- **Education:** Bachelor's degree in Environmental Science, Biology, Ecology, or related field
- **Experience:** 1-3 years experience in environmental consulting, government, or industry (graduate applications welcome)
- **Technical Skills:** Proficiency in environmental monitoring techniques, GIS software, and data analysis tools
- **Field Capability:** Comfortable working in remote mining environments, valid driver's licence, willingness to travel
- **Communication:** Strong written and verbal communication skills for preparing technical reports and engaging with diverse stakeholders
- **Collaboration:** Ability to work effectively with operations teams, Traditional Owners, and regulatory agencies
- **Values Alignment:** Commitment to safety, environmental stewardship, and respectful engagement with Indigenous communities

**Preferred Qualifications:**
- Experience with mining industry environmental management
- Knowledge of WA environmental legislation and approval processes
- Familiarity with rehabilitation and closure planning

## What We Offer

- **Competitive Package:** Base salary $75,000-$85,000 plus superannuation, reviewed annually
- **Professional Development:** Study assistance program, conference attendance, and mentoring from Dr. Mehta's experienced team
- **Work-Life Balance:** Flexible working arrangements, 4 weeks annual leave plus Christmas shutdown
- **Travel Opportunities:** Site allowances for field work, exposure to cutting-edge autonomous mining operations
- **Career Growth:** Clear progression pathways within our expanding critical minerals portfolio
- **Health & Wellbeing:** Comprehensive health insurance, EAP services, and wellness initiatives
- **Purpose-Driven Work:** Contribute to Australia's clean energy transition while maintaining rigorous environmental standards

## How to Apply

Ready to make your mark in the mining industry's sustainable future? Send your CV and cover letter to careers@ironvaleresources.com.au, referencing "Environmental Scientist - ENV001" in the subject line.

Applications close **Friday, 15 March 2024**. We encourage applications from Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander candidates and are committed to creating an inclusive workplace that reflects the diversity of our communities.

*IronVale Resources is an equal opportunity employer committed to safety, sustainability, and community partnership.*
jobs.manager_persona:
You are Dr. Ravi Mehta, General Manager — Sustainability & Environment at IronVale Resources.

IronVale Resources is A mid-tier iron ore and lithium mining company operating two
active mine sites in the Pilbara and one lithium project in the
Goldfields-Esperance region. Headquartered in Perth with FIFO
workforce rotating to site. Known for strong safety culture,
community engagement with Traditional Owners, and early adoption
of autonomous mining technologies.


ABOUT YOU:
PhD in environmental geochemistry from UWA. Joined from the
WA Department of Mines after six years in regulatory roles.
Manages environmental compliance, rehabilitation programs,
and the increasingly complex stakeholder relationships around
the new lithium project. A scientist in a mining company,
often bridging two very different cultures.


PERSONALITY:

- Meticulous and thorough

- Calm under pressure

- Leads by example

- Knowledge-sharing

- Standards-driven


- Measured and evidence-based

- Patient with non-technical stakeholders

- Passionate about genuine environmental outcomes


YOUR KNOWLEDGE AREAS:

- Domain-specific technical expertise

- Industry best practices

- Project execution and delivery

- Quality assurance

- Mentoring junior staff

- Troubleshooting and problem resolution


- WA environmental approval processes

- Mine closure and rehabilitation

- Traditional Owner consultation protocols

- Critical minerals supply chain sustainability


COMMUNICATION STYLE:
Precise and knowledgeable. Shares expertise generously but knows limits.
Practical and hands-on. Values preparation and planning. Can explain
technical concepts clearly.


CURRENT SITUATION:
IronVale's iron ore operations are mature and profitable, but
the board has committed to diversifying into critical minerals.
The Goldfields lithium project is approaching final investment
decision, requiring a fundamentally different skill set from
iron ore. Meanwhile, the autonomous haulage trial at Pilbara
East is disrupting established workforce patterns, creating
tension between innovation advocates and the operations team
who value proven methods. The company needs to manage a cultural
shift while maintaining the safety record that defines its
reputation.


YOUR PERSPECTIVE: The lithium project is exciting scientifically but the
environmental approvals timeline is being compressed by
commercial pressure. Worried about cutting corners in
Traditional Owner consultation. Sees autonomous mining as
positive for environmental outcomes but needs data.



REFERRALS:

- For operations questions, suggest speaking with Karen Whitfield

- For community questions, suggest speaking with Danielle Keenan

- For finance questions, suggest speaking with Tom Gallagher



YOUR OPINIONS (express these naturally when relevant):

- The lithium project approvals are more complex than anyone in the boardroom realises

- Our rehabilitation record is genuinely good — we should be prouder of it

- Heritage agreements aren't boxes to tick — they're relationships to maintain

- ESG reporting is becoming as important as production reports



RULES:
1. Stay in character as Dr. Ravi Mehta at all times
2. Only discuss what you would realistically know as General Manager — Sustainability & Environment
3. Never fabricate specific dates, prices, or commitments you haven't been told about
4. If unsure, say so honestly — real employees don't know everything
5. You are speaking with a student consultant/auditor — be helpful but realistic
6. Do not break character or acknowledge being an AI


ADDITIONAL CONTEXT:
The lithium project is exciting scientifically but the
environmental approvals timeline is being compressed by
commercial pressure. Worried about cutting corners in
Traditional Owner consultation. Sees autonomous mining as
positive for environmental outcomes but needs data.

jobs.title: Finance Analyst — Project Evaluation
jobs.description:
# Finance Analyst — Project Evaluation

## About the Role

IronVale Resources is seeking a motivated Finance Analyst to join our Finance team and support the evaluation of our transformational Goldfields lithium project. This graduate-level position offers the opportunity to work directly on one of Western Australia's most significant critical minerals developments, contributing to financial modelling that will inform our final investment decision.

Reporting to Chief Financial Officer Tom Gallagher, you'll be part of a tight-knit finance team of six professionals supporting IronVale's evolution from a pure-play iron ore producer to a diversified critical minerals company. This role provides exceptional exposure to senior leadership and board-level decision making while developing core financial analysis skills in the dynamic resources sector.

## Key Responsibilities

- Develop and maintain comprehensive financial models for the Goldfields lithium project, incorporating capital expenditure, operating costs, and revenue projections
- Conduct sensitivity analysis and scenario planning to assess project risks and returns under various market conditions
- Support capital budgeting processes across IronVale's portfolio, comparing investment opportunities between iron ore optimisation and lithium development
- Prepare detailed financial presentations and briefing materials for executive leadership, board meetings, and potential investors
- Collaborate with our engineering and operations teams to validate cost assumptions and operational parameters
- Monitor lithium market trends and pricing forecasts to inform project economics
- Assist with quarterly financial reporting and variance analysis for existing operations
- Support due diligence activities and engage with external advisors including investment banks and independent engineers

## What You'll Bring

- Bachelor's degree in Finance, Accounting, Economics, or related field (graduated within the last 2 years)
- Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Excel, including financial modelling, scenario analysis, and data visualisation
- Understanding of discounted cash flow analysis, NPV/IRR calculations, and capital budgeting principles
- Strong analytical mindset with attention to detail and ability to work with large datasets
- Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with experience preparing executive-level presentations
- Interest in the resources sector and critical minerals value chain
- Ability to work collaboratively in a fast-paced environment while managing competing priorities
- Australian working rights and willingness to occasionally travel to project sites

*Preferred but not essential: Experience with financial software such as @RISK or Crystal Ball for Monte Carlo analysis, CFA Level 1 candidacy, or internship experience in resources, investment banking, or corporate finance.*

## What We Offer

- Competitive graduate salary package of $75,000 - $85,000 plus superannuation
- Comprehensive graduate development program with structured mentoring and rotation opportunities
- Direct exposure to major project development and M&A activities
- Professional development support including CFA study assistance and conference attendance
- Flexible working arrangements with hybrid work-from-home options
- Health and wellness benefits including private health insurance subsidy
- Opportunity to visit our operational sites and understand the full mining value chain
- Clear progression pathway to Senior Analyst and Finance Manager roles
- Participation in IronVale's equity incentive scheme after 12 months

## How to Apply

Submit your application including a tailored cover letter and resume through our careers portal. Applications close Friday, 15 December 2024.

For questions about this role, contact our People & Community team at careers@ironvaleresources.com.au.

IronVale Resources is an equal opportunity employer committed to building a diverse and inclusive workforce. We encourage applications from Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples, and value the perspectives that different backgrounds bring to our team.

*Please note: This position may require occasional site visits to our Goldfields project location and periodic travel to our Perth head office.*
jobs.manager_persona:
You are Tom Gallagher, Chief Financial Officer at IronVale Resources.

IronVale Resources is A mid-tier iron ore and lithium mining company operating two
active mine sites in the Pilbara and one lithium project in the
Goldfields-Esperance region. Headquartered in Perth with FIFO
workforce rotating to site. Known for strong safety culture,
community engagement with Traditional Owners, and early adoption
of autonomous mining technologies.


ABOUT YOU:
Joined from a Big Four firm where he specialised in mining
sector advisory. Manages financial planning, investor
relations, and the capital allocation decisions that will
determine whether the lithium project gets the green light.
The board listens to his numbers.


PERSONALITY:

- Analytical and pragmatic

- Transparent about numbers

- Risk-aware

- Systems-focused

- Cautious but supportive of growth


- Analytical and commercially sharp

- Comfortable presenting to boards and investors

- Conservative with capital


YOUR KNOWLEDGE AREAS:

- Financial reporting and analysis

- Budget management and forecasting

- Cash flow management

- Cost control and profitability

- Financial compliance and audit

- Pricing strategy


- Mining project financial modelling

- ASX reporting and compliance

- Capital raising and investor relations

- Iron ore and lithium commodity markets


COMMUNICATION STYLE:
Data-driven and precise. Frames discussions in terms of financial impact.
Honest about constraints and risks. Uses specific numbers and percentages
where appropriate. Practical rather than theoretical.


CURRENT SITUATION:
IronVale's iron ore operations are mature and profitable, but
the board has committed to diversifying into critical minerals.
The Goldfields lithium project is approaching final investment
decision, requiring a fundamentally different skill set from
iron ore. Meanwhile, the autonomous haulage trial at Pilbara
East is disrupting established workforce patterns, creating
tension between innovation advocates and the operations team
who value proven methods. The company needs to manage a cultural
shift while maintaining the safety record that defines its
reputation.


YOUR PERSPECTIVE: Crunching the numbers on the lithium FID. The project NPV
is positive but sensitive to lithium price assumptions.
Wants the board to understand the funding gap between iron
ore cash flow and lithium capex. Not against growth, but
insists on financial discipline.



REFERRALS:

- For operations questions, suggest speaking with Karen Whitfield

- For environment questions, suggest speaking with Dr. Ravi Mehta



YOUR OPINIONS (express these naturally when relevant):

- Iron ore is paying the bills — we protect that revenue stream

- The lithium business case works at current prices but margins are thin

- Autonomous haulage ROI is compelling but the capex is front-loaded

- We can't fund everything — the board needs to prioritise



RULES:
1. Stay in character as Tom Gallagher at all times
2. Only discuss what you would realistically know as Chief Financial Officer
3. Never fabricate specific dates, prices, or commitments you haven't been told about
4. If unsure, say so honestly — real employees don't know everything
5. You are speaking with a student consultant/auditor — be helpful but realistic
6. Do not break character or acknowledge being an AI


ADDITIONAL CONTEXT:
Crunching the numbers on the lithium FID. The project NPV
is positive but sensitive to lithium price assumptions.
Wants the board to understand the funding gap between iron
ore cash flow and lithium capex. Not against growth, but
insists on financial discipline.

jobs.title: Graduate Mining Engineer
jobs.description:
# Graduate Mining Engineer

**IronVale Resources | Mine Operations | Perth, Western Australia | Graduate Program**

## About the Role

Join IronVale Resources as a Graduate Mining Engineer and kick-start your career at our flagship Pilbara East iron ore operation. This 18-month graduate program offers comprehensive exposure to modern mining operations through structured rotations across drill-and-blast, mine planning, and production teams. You'll work directly with experienced engineers and operators while contributing to real projects that impact our daily production of 2.5 million tonnes annually.

Reporting to Site Manager Pete Drummond, you'll be based at our Pilbara East site on a 2-weeks-on/1-week-off FIFO roster from Perth. This role suits engineering graduates seeking hands-on experience in a high-performing mining environment where safety, innovation, and operational excellence drive everything we do.

As IronVale transitions toward critical minerals with our upcoming Goldfields lithium project, you'll gain exposure to diverse mining methodologies and be positioned for exciting career progression opportunities across our expanding operations.

## Key Responsibilities

**Drill & Blast Rotation (6 months)**
- Support blast design and pattern optimization under supervision of senior blast engineers
- Conduct pre-blast inspections and post-blast assessments
- Assist with explosive inventory management and regulatory compliance
- Participate in autonomous haulage integration planning around blast zones

**Mine Planning Rotation (6 months)**
- Develop short-term production schedules using MineSched and Surpac software
- Support grade control sampling programs and reconciliation analysis
- Assist with pit optimization studies and waste dump design
- Contribute to resource definition drilling programs

**Production Engineering Rotation (6 months)**
- Monitor daily production KPIs and identify improvement opportunities
- Support equipment performance analysis and maintenance planning
- Participate in autonomous truck trial data analysis and optimization
- Assist with new technology implementation and change management initiatives

**Cross-functional Responsibilities**
- Maintain rigorous safety standards and contribute to our Zero Harm culture
- Support environmental monitoring and compliance activities
- Participate in Traditional Owner engagement and cultural heritage protocols
- Contribute to graduate cohort projects and knowledge sharing sessions

## What You'll Bring

**Essential Requirements**
- Bachelor's degree in Mining Engineering or related engineering discipline
- Australian work rights (citizenship or permanent residency)
- Current drivers license and ability to obtain Heavy Vehicle License
- Strong analytical skills with proficiency in Excel and basic programming concepts
- Excellent communication skills for diverse stakeholder engagement
- Physical fitness for mine site operations and FIFO lifestyle
- Commitment to safety-first mindset and continuous learning

**Highly Regarded**
- Vacation work or internship experience in mining or heavy industry
- Familiarity with mining software (Surpac, MineSched, or similar)
- Understanding of autonomous vehicle technology or digital mining concepts
- Experience working with Indigenous communities or cultural awareness training
- Leadership experience through university projects, sports, or community involvement

## What We Offer

**Graduate Program Benefits**
- Competitive graduate salary package: $85,000 + superannuation + site allowances
- Comprehensive 18-month structured learning program with dedicated mentorship
- All flights, accommodation, and meals covered during site rotations
- Professional development allowance ($2,500 annually for training and conferences)

**Career Development**
- Clear progression pathways to senior engineering and management roles
- Exposure to cutting-edge autonomous mining technology implementation
- Cross-commodity experience with potential lithium project involvement
- Industry-leading safety training including underground confined space and explosives handling

**Lifestyle & Wellbeing**
- FIFO roster providing excellent work-life balance (2/1 swing)
- Perth CBD office accommodation during R&R periods
- Comprehensive health and wellbeing programs
- Mental health support and counseling services
- Annual team building events and graduate cohort networking

**Company Culture**
- Join a close-knit team of 180 employees where your contribution matters
- Work with Traditional Owners on meaningful land management partnerships
- Be part of Australia's critical minerals future through our lithium diversification
- Contribute to industry-leading environmental rehabilitation programs

## How to Apply

Submit your application including cover letter, academic transcripts, and CV through our careers portal. Applications close **Friday, March 15, 2024**.

We'll be attending the WA Mining Engineering Career Fair at Curtin University on March 8th - come meet Pete Drummond and other team members to learn more about life at IronVale.

Successful candidates will participate in a two-stage interview process including technical assessment and site visit. Final offers will be made by April 30th for a July 2024 program commencement.

IronVale Resources is an equal opportunity employer committed to diversity and inclusion. We particularly encourage applications from women, Indigenous Australians, and candidates from diverse backgrounds.

*Please note: This role requires medical clearance including drug and alcohol testing, and security clearance for site access.*
jobs.manager_persona:
You are Pete Drummond, Site Manager — Pilbara East at IronVale Resources.

IronVale Resources is A mid-tier iron ore and lithium mining company operating two
active mine sites in the Pilbara and one lithium project in the
Goldfields-Esperance region. Headquartered in Perth with FIFO
workforce rotating to site. Known for strong safety culture,
community engagement with Traditional Owners, and early adoption
of autonomous mining technologies.


ABOUT YOU:
Runs the larger of IronVale's two Pilbara iron ore mines.
Started as a drill-and-blast engineer and worked up to site
manager. The autonomous haulage trial is happening on his
site, making him ground zero for the technology transition.
Respected by the FIFO workforce for being a practical,
hands-on leader.


PERSONALITY:

- Organised and systematic

- Calm under pressure

- Detail-oriented

- Team-focused

- Practical problem-solver


- Straight-talking and practical

- Protective of his crew

- Skeptical of Perth-driven initiatives


YOUR KNOWLEDGE AREAS:

- Day-to-day operations and workflows

- Team management and scheduling

- Process development and improvement

- Vendor and supplier relationships

- Quality control and standards

- Resource allocation

- Problem-solving and crisis management


- Open-pit iron ore mining operations

- Haul truck fleet management

- FIFO roster management

- Dust and noise management


COMMUNICATION STYLE:
Clear and practical in explanations. Focuses on processes and systems.
Provides actionable information. Realistic about challenges and constraints.
Shares operational insights from experience.


CURRENT SITUATION:
IronVale's iron ore operations are mature and profitable, but
the board has committed to diversifying into critical minerals.
The Goldfields lithium project is approaching final investment
decision, requiring a fundamentally different skill set from
iron ore. Meanwhile, the autonomous haulage trial at Pilbara
East is disrupting established workforce patterns, creating
tension between innovation advocates and the operations team
who value proven methods. The company needs to manage a cultural
shift while maintaining the safety record that defines its
reputation.


YOUR PERSPECTIVE: On the front line of the autonomous haulage trial. Personally
supportive of the technology but dealing with workforce anxiety
and union questions daily. Wants Perth to communicate better
about what autonomy means for jobs. Feels caught between
innovation pressure from above and loyalty to his crew.



REFERRALS:

- For operations questions, suggest speaking with Karen Whitfield

- For safety questions, suggest speaking with Brooke Lawson

- For HR questions, suggest speaking with Danielle Keenan



YOUR OPINIONS (express these naturally when relevant):

- Perth can have all the strategies they want — we deliver the tonnes

- Autonomous trucks work in theory but my operators need real answers about their jobs

- Every hour of downtime costs us real money

- The best innovations come from the people doing the work



RULES:
1. Stay in character as Pete Drummond at all times
2. Only discuss what you would realistically know as Site Manager — Pilbara East
3. Never fabricate specific dates, prices, or commitments you haven't been told about
4. If unsure, say so honestly — real employees don't know everything
5. You are speaking with a student consultant/auditor — be helpful but realistic
6. Do not break character or acknowledge being an AI


ADDITIONAL CONTEXT:
On the front line of the autonomous haulage trial. Personally
supportive of the technology but dealing with workforce anxiety
and union questions daily. Wants Perth to communicate better
about what autonomy means for jobs. Feels caught between
innovation pressure from above and loyalty to his crew.

additional_postings.title: Graduate Mining Engineer | Top Tier WA Iron Ore Producer
additional_postings.description: WestForce Staffing is exclusively engaged with one of Western Australia's premier mid-tier iron ore producers to recruit a Graduate Mining Engineer. Based in Perth with FIFO rotations to a Pilbara operation, this role offers exposure to autonomous haulage technology, traditional mining methods, and a respected safety culture. Our client offers strong graduate development pathways and competitive remuneration. Company name disclosed to shortlisted candidates.

task_templates.title: Board briefing note — autonomous haulage trial
task_templates.brief: One-page briefing note for the board on the current autonomous haulage trial — status, early findings, what's next.
task_templates.description: The board is reviewing the autonomous haulage trial at next month's meeting. Draft a one-page briefing note covering: current status of the trial, 2-3 early findings (good or bad), any safety observations, and what decision the board is actually being asked to make. Assume the board is commercially experienced but not operationally deep — translate jargon. Keep it tight: one page, scannable, honest.

task_templates.title: Haul truck cycle time analysis
task_templates.brief: Analyse haul truck cycle time data and identify three efficiency improvements worth trialling.
task_templates.description: Operations has shared a month of haul truck cycle time data (load, haul, dump, return, queue). Analyse the patterns and identify three specific efficiency improvements we could trial. For each, explain: what you observed in the data, what you'd change, the expected impact (even if rough), and any risk the change introduces. Don't just list obvious textbook improvements — tell us what this particular dataset is pointing at. Numbers beat adjectives.

task_templates.title: Stakeholder communication plan — Goldfields lithium project
task_templates.brief: Draft a stakeholder communication plan for the Goldfields lithium project Traditional Owner consultation.
task_templates.description: IronVale is progressing the Goldfields lithium project and Traditional Owner consultation is the most important relationship we have. Draft a stakeholder communication plan covering: (1) the specific groups we need to engage and in what order, (2) the mode and cadence of engagement for each group, (3) the topics we should lead with (and the ones we should not dodge), (4) how we'll know consultation is genuinely working, and (5) the red lines — what would cause us to pause or change course. Assume anything you write could be read back to us in a land council meeting. Write accordingly.
